action.reasoning: Observation: Iter 10 eval failed because predict.py raised FileNotFoundError when lat.npy or lon.npy missing in /data. The eval container may not provide these files even though documentation says they are available. Analysis: We must make predict.py robust to missing lat.npy/lon.npy by computing the latitude and longitude arrays from known grid parameters (5.625° resolution, 32x64). The model checkpoint expects 8 input channels (6 data + 2 positional). We can infer in_channels from the state_dict, and if 8, always add positional channels using either loaded files or computed arrays. Decision: Update predict.py to compute lat/lon when files are missing, using np.linspace to generate lat from -87.1875 to 87.1875 (32 points) and lon from 0 to 354.375 (64 points). Keep all other logic unchanged. Then resubmit eval for the iter 8 checkpoint, which has the best validation metrics so far. Remaining time 0.69h is enough for eval + possibly a small training improvement if the score is promising.
